text.title: api-gateway-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for api-gateway-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Disabling individual linter-like rules inside the gateway (e.g., rate-limit exceptions for "power users") instead of handling it properly in configuration or a BFF.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Ad-hoc exceptions creep in and become policy debt. Use configuration (rate-limit plans per consumer type) or a BFF service for complex routing logic.

failure-mode.description: Agents put business logic in Kong plugins (Lua) instead of a dedicated BFF service.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Push back hard. Gateway plugins are for cross-cutting concerns (auth, rate-limit, tracing). Business logic belongs in a service layer.

failure-mode.description: Agents generate gateway YAML but no smoke tests. A typo'd path silently 404s in production.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require agents to pair every route change with a smoke test: curl script or Pact contract test that proves the route, auth, and rate-limit behave.

failure-mode.description: Agents forget to enable preserve_host on Kong, breaking apps that rely on the original Host header for tenant routing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always include preserve_host: true in route config, or document why it is not needed.

failure-mode.description: Agents configure rate-limiting per-IP behind a CDN, where every request comes from the CDN's IPs — effective rate-limit is per-CDN, not per-user.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require agents to check whether the gateway is behind a CDN or load balancer. If so, rate-limit by consumer ID or X-Forwarded-For, never by remote IP.

failure-mode.description: AWS API Gateway: agents miss the difference between proxy+ (passthrough) and per-method routing. Usage plans need API keys + stages, easy to misconfigure.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Provide agents with a template serverless.yml or Terraform module that handles these details correctly.

failure-mode.description: Agents generate gateway config without a load test. A change to rate limits could cut throughput in half and nobody notices until the product team complains.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require a human checkpoint: every gateway change should be validated against a load test against staging (e.g., hey or vegeta) so cost/throughput impact is verified before production deploy.
